rationale: Dogecoin has no supply cap and emits a fixed 10,000 DOGE per block (one block ~1 minute), yielding ~5.256 billion new DOGE per year. Dividing that fixed annual emission by the current circulating supply of ~147.6 billion DOGE (per CoinGecko as of mid-July 2025) gives an annualized inflation rate of approximately 3.66%; because the numerator is fixed while the denominator (circulating supply) grows each year, the percentage rate declines monotonically over time.
